As a text data management task, the automation potential is very high, leaving the technician to supervise complex cases.

Dietetic technicians design menus and supervise nutrition in healthcare or school settings, drawing on digital nutritional data. AI already generates personalised meal plans that meet specific medical and caloric requirements instantly. The technician maintains relevance in the physical supervision of diet preparation and in direct nutritional counselling to patients in the clinical setting.

These technicians assist in visual examinations and the assembly of optical solutions, integrating digital technology and customer service. AI automates visual acuity tests and suggests the best lens options based on examination data autonomously. The physical fitting of frames and personal interaction with the client to ensure their visual comfort are the tasks that protect the human technical role.

Physiotherapy assistants support the application of physical treatments and rehabilitation exercises under supervision. AI can guide patients through simple exercises via computer vision systems, but it cannot apply massages or manual techniques. Physical assistance with the mobility of patients who have difficulties and motivational support during therapy require a constant human physical presence and strength.

nombre: Occupational risk prevention and environmental health technicians
justificacion: These technicians supervise occupational safety and environmental quality through physical inspections and digital data monitoring. AI analyses accident records and pollution data to propose preventive measures automatically. The inspectorial nature of the work, which requires visiting construction sites and industrial plants to physically verify compliance with EU regulations, ensures human continuity.

Veterinary assistants support the physical care and diagnostic testing of animals in clinics or on farms. AI facilitates laboratory sample analysis and monitors animal health through intelligent wearable devices. The physical handling of animals for examinations, facility hygiene, and support during surgical procedures require manual dexterity and a human physical presence.

This diverse group of health technicians performs clinical support and diagnostic tasks in hybrid hospital environments. AI automates medical data processing and improves the precision of digital diagnostic equipment on a generalised basis. Direct interaction with patients and the execution of manual clinical protocols in the real hospital setting preserve the need for these technical support professionals.

nombre: Acupuncture, naturopathy, homeopathy, traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurveda practitioners
justificacion: These professionals apply therapies based on physical stimulation and the patient's energy balance, requiring deep manual contact. Although AI can recommend treatments based on symptoms, the therapeutic practice depends entirely on the therapist's tactile sensitivity and intuitive judgement. The strong component of belief and the personal therapist-patient relationship in traditional medicine protect this sector from automation.

This group includes various alternative therapists who use manual and emotional accompaniment techniques in a physical and relational setting. AI cannot replicate the warmth of human care or the adaptive physical manipulation required for these non-conventional therapies. Demand for these professions is based precisely on the client's desire to receive personalised human attention outside of technologised environments.

nombre: Financial and stock exchange support professionals and intermediaries
justificacion: Their work consists of intermediating in financial and currency markets, a purely digital activity based on information flows. Algorithmic AI and high-frequency trading already execute buy-sell orders autonomously in milliseconds, surpassing any human capability. The professional is evolving towards a relationship management and risk supervision role, as pure technical intermediation is massively automated.

Loan officers assess creditworthiness and sell credit products, operating in a digital risk analysis environment. AI already performs credit scoring automatically and personalises banking offers based on the client's digital history with high precision. The human factor remains in the management of complex real estate transactions (mortgages) and in negotiations with corporate clients that require direct personal engagement.

Bookkeepers record the daily financial transactions of businesses, a digital task of data entry and classification. AI, through automatic invoice processing and direct bank integration, already automates nearly all basic bookkeeping without human intervention. This profession is undergoing a transformation towards management control, due to the extreme vulnerability of manual data recording.

nombre: Statistical, mathematical and related support professionals
justificacion: Their work is to support statistical and mathematical analysis through the use of specialised software and digital data processing. AI and auto-ML tools already perform data cleaning, complex statistical calculations, and visualisations autonomously from massive files. The human supervision component is limited to data quality verification and the contextual interpretation of the statistical results obtained.

Valuers determine the worth of assets based on physical inspections and comparative market data analysis. AI uses automated valuation models (AVM) to estimate the price of properties and vehicles with great precision based on digital big data. Physical inspection to detect hidden defects, the actual state of conservation, and the legal certification of value in the Spanish market still require a human expert on-site.

Commercial agents act as intermediaries in B2B sales, requiring strong social intelligence and information management in digital environments. AI assists in identifying potential clients and personalises sales proposals based on market behaviour data. However, the agent's success depends on direct negotiation, personal trust, and a physical network of contacts, elements that AI cannot build autonomously.

Insurance agents sell policies and advise on risk, a task that blends digital analysis with commercial persuasion. AI already automates the underwriting of simple risks and the management of minor claims through computer vision and intelligent chatbots. The sale of complex business insurance and the personalised management of major claims require human mediation to negotiate terms and provide reassurance to the client.

Their work consists of negotiating with suppliers and managing the supply of goods in a purely digital and logistical environment. AI optimises supplier selection based on price, lead times, and historical quality, automating much of the routine purchasing process. The negotiation of long-term strategic contracts and the resolution of crises in the global supply chain maintain the need for a human purchasing agent.

Shipping agents manage the formalities and logistics of vessels in port, a digital administrative task critical to maritime trade. AI automates the management of port documents and the coordination of stevedoring and supply services through integrated digital platforms. The on-site presence at the port to supervise physical operations and resolve unforeseen problems with the crew or customs protects the human role.

Customs representatives manage the clearance of goods at borders, drawing on highly digitalised legal and tariff regulations. AI systems already automate tariff classification and the verification of customs documents to expedite international trade. The legal liability before the Spanish Tax Agency and the physical management of on-site customs inspections ensure that the human professional remains indispensable.

Event organisation requires digital logistical planning and high-intensity relational physical on-site execution. AI helps with agenda design, registration management, and the personalisation of the attendee experience through intelligent applications. The physical coordination of suppliers at the event venue and the immediate resolution of real logistical problems depend on the operational capability of a human organiser.

nombre: Labour contracting agents and intermediaries (except entertainment agents)
justificacion: These agents mediate in labour recruitment, analysing profiles and company needs in digital job-search environments. AI already filters candidates through competency analysis and predicts cultural fit for positions with great speed. Human value centres on the in-depth personal interview, salary negotiation, and the recruitment of executive talent that cannot be found in public databases.

Estate agents manage the sale and rental of properties, combining digital marketing with physical property viewings. AI generates virtual tours, optimises sale prices, and automates the drafting of contracts and property listings efficiently. The success of the transaction depends on commercial skill during the in-person viewing, empathy with buyers, and the final negotiation between the parties.

Spokespersons manage public communications and media relations, drawing on oratory and social intelligence in digital environments. AI can draft press releases and analyse the impact of communications on social media, but it cannot replace the credibility of a human figure. Responding to uncomfortable questions on live broadcasts and managing public trust require a human authenticity that AI cannot emulate.

nombre: Artistic and sports agents and other commercial service agents n.e.c.
justificacion: Artistic and sports agents mediate in complex professional careers, drawing on negotiation and personal networks of contacts. AI assists by analysing the market value of talents and monitoring their digital impact in real time exhaustively. The emotional management of the artist or athlete and the negotiation of high-exclusivity contracts depend on a human bond of trust and a personal strategic vision.

Secretarial supervisors coordinate administrative workflows and support teams in purely digital office environments. AI and autonomous software agents already organise diaries, manage emails, and supervise the completion of routine administrative tasks. The role is evolving towards project management and hybrid team coordination, as technical administrative supervision is being automated by intelligent CRMs.

Legal assistants support lawyers and notaries in the preparation of documents and case file management in digital legal environments. Generative AI is already capable of drafting procedural briefs, summarising case law, and organising complex trial documentation with technical precision. As a task based on the processing of normative texts, its vulnerability to automation by language models is very high.

Executive assistants manage the diary and communications of senior executives, a digital task requiring high trust and organisational skill. AI assists by drafting communication drafts, summarising meetings, and planning travel autonomously. The value of the human assistant lies in the deep knowledge of the executive's personal priorities, absolute discretion, and the ability to resolve complex unforeseen situations requiring human judgement.

Clinical secretaries manage appointments and medical documentation in highly digitalised administrative settings with physical patient contact. AI already automates appointment bookings, treatment reminders, and the management of digital medical records through chatbots and voice systems. The in-person reception of patients in a vulnerable state and the resolution of administrative incidents at the health centre maintain a critical human component.

These professionals manage taxation within the Spanish Public Administration, working with fiscal regulations and massive digital databases. The Tax Agency's AI already detects fraud and automates tax assessments for the majority of standard taxpayers very efficiently. The human specialist is needed for the resolution of complex appeals and for advice on atypical tax situations that require interpretation of the law.

nombre: Public administration social services support professionals
justificacion: Their work consists of technical support for public social services, managing case files and aid applications in digital environments. AI can automate the initial triage of applications and the verification of compliance with financial requirements instantly. Direct interaction with citizens in situations of social exclusion and the technical assessment of cases requiring social sensitivity protect the need for the human professional.

nombre: Public administration licensing services support professionals
justificacion: These professionals manage the issuance of public licences and permits, a purely administrative task based on digital regulations. Spain's electronic administration already allows the automated granting of standard licences through algorithmic verification of requirements. The human role is reserved for the physical inspection of licence compliance and the resolution of cases presenting unforeseen legal or technical conflicts.

nombre: Other public administration support professionals for inspection and control
justificacion: This group performs inspection and administrative control tasks across various sectors, combining data analysis with physical verification. AI systems facilitate the selection of inspection targets based on risk profiles and digitally monitor regulatory compliance. The legal authority to conduct on-site inspections and issue infringement reports is a sovereign function that EU regulations reserve for humans.

nombre: National, regional and local police technicians
justificacion: Police technicians perform support tasks in criminal investigation and public security, integrating advanced digital technology and fieldwork. AI assists in digital forensic analysis, biometric identification, and the prediction of crime risk zones through data analytics. The execution of arrests, the obtaining of testimonies, and physical intervention in dangerous situations require an irreplaceable human presence and authority.

Civil Guard non-commissioned officers lead security and surveillance operations in a complex physical and rural environment with digital technical support. AI monitors critical infrastructure and analyses traffic through computer vision to automatically detect suspicious behaviour. On-site command, territorial knowledge, and the capacity for armed physical response to serious crimes ensure the continuity of this human operational role.

nombre: Legal and similar services support professionals
justificacion: These professionals assist with legal procedures and mediation, operating in an environment purely based on information and digital communication. Generative AI can draft mediation documents and advise on standard legal procedures based on current legislation with great accuracy. Lacking the signing authority of a lawyer or notary, their support tasks are highly susceptible to being absorbed by legal AI agents.

Private detectives conduct investigations and surveillance to obtain evidence, combining digital tracking with discreet physical tailing. AI facilitates open-source intelligence (OSINT) and facial or licence plate recognition in video recordings automatically. Nevertheless, mobile physical surveillance in the real world and the ability to obtain information through human social engineering are tasks that AI cannot emulate.

nombre: Social work and social education support professionals
justificacion: Their work consists of supporting integration and social education programmes through direct care for groups in physical community settings. AI can help manage activities and monitor user progress through data, but social intervention is inherently human. Empathy, street-level accompaniment, and the building of neighbourhood trust networks are functions that require the professional's in-person presence.

These promoters design and implement equality actions in local settings, drawing on social communication and training. AI assists by generating awareness-raising materials and analysing demographic data to identify structural inequalities quickly. The promoter's success depends on social mobilisation, the delivery of in-person workshops, and local political advocacy, tasks that are intrinsically human and relational.

Community animators foster social participation and organise cultural activities in physical neighbourhood settings. Their work is intensely relational and in-person, requiring skills in group facilitation and community conflict resolution. AI cannot replicate human enthusiasm or the ability to adapt festive or educational activities to the emotional and social climate of a specific neighbourhood in real time.

Lay auxiliaries support the activities of religious organisations, combining administrative management with physical service to the community. AI can automate event organisation and communication with the faithful through digital channels efficiently. The value of the position lies in personal accompaniment, physical participation in rites, and direct charitable assistance, aspects that maintain the essential human component.

Professional athletes perform an extreme physical activity based on bodily performance and competition in the real world. This occupation is intrinsically human; AI is only used as a tool for performance analysis, diet optimisation, and injury prevention. The economic and social value of professional sport lies in the effort, epic narrative, and human physical talent, making automation conceptually impossible.

Coaches and referees manage athletic performance and rule enforcement in physical competitive environments. AI is already used to analyse rival tactics (sports big data) and assist in officiating (VAR, automatic offside detection). The motivational leadership of the team, the management of athletes' egos, and human judgement in ambiguous or high-tension game situations maintain the relevance of these in-person roles.

Sports instructors teach physical technique and train athletes in gyms or sports centres, requiring constant in-person supervision. AI can offer personalised routines and correct simple postures through computer vision on mobile devices. However, motivation during physical exertion, manual technique correction, and on-site prevention of traumatic injuries require a human instructor to be present.

Recreation monitors organise games and leisure activities for groups in physical holiday or educational settings. The work is based on personal energy, the physical safety of participants, and the capacity for live social entertainment. AI cannot replace the joy of shared play or the responsibility of supervising children or adults in dynamic physical environments such as pools, camps, or parks.

Photography combines artistic vision with technical mastery of light and composition in physical or studio environments. AI already performs spectacular automatic edits, generates photorealistic images from scratch, and enhances the quality of old photos remarkably. The human photographer retains value in the capture of unique spontaneous moments (photojournalism, weddings), model direction, and a personal and unrepeatable creative eye.

Interior designers project habitable spaces, integrating digital aesthetics with the management of materials and physical works. Generative AI already creates stunning interior design renders and optimises furniture layout according to space instantly. The human designer is key for client dialogue, the supervision of the physical renovation on-site, and the tactile knowledge of the quality of real materials.

nombre: Art gallery, museum and library technicians
justificacion: These technicians manage collections and exhibitions in physical cultural settings such as museums and art galleries. AI automates the cataloguing of works and personalises the visitor experience through intelligent guides and augmented reality. The physical mounting of exhibitions, the careful handling of art pieces, and the management of the physical security of collections protect the human operational component of this occupation.

Chefs lead professional kitchens, design menus, and supervise the physical execution of dishes in high-pressure environments. AI helps create molecular flavour combinations and optimises recipe costs through market price analysis. However, kitchen team leadership, the final sensory judgement (taste and smell), and artistic culinary creativity are human capabilities that define haute cuisine.

nombre: Other cultural and artistic activities technicians and support professionals
justificacion: This group includes various technical support professionals in culture and art who perform technical and operational tasks in hybrid settings. AI assists in technical production and in the rapid and efficient generation of artistic support materials. The physical presence for the assembly of artistic productions and the resolution of technical problems during live performances ensure that the human professional maintains operational relevance against digital automation.

IT operations technicians supervise and maintain servers and data centre systems in purely technological environments. AI and AIOps already automate hardware fault detection, software deployment, and load balancing autonomously. Being a natively digital environment, most routine supervision is now algorithmic, leaving the technician with crisis management and the physical maintenance of hardware.

User technical support helps resolve hardware and software problems in corporate or domestic environments. AI, through voice systems and expert chatbots, already resolves the majority of common Level 1 incidents instantly. The profession is highly exposed, although the human technician remains necessary for physical device repairs and for assisting users who require a pedagogical or in-person explanation.

Network technicians install and configure communication infrastructure, combining physical cabling with the digital configuration of routers and switches. AI optimises the network automatically and detects traffic bottlenecks without human intervention. The need to carry out the physical deployment of cabling, antennas, and network equipment in buildings and outdoors ensures the continuity of this manual and operational technical work.

Web technicians maintain websites and online applications, handling deployment and security in purely digital environments. AI already automates vulnerability detection, page-load speed optimisation, and software dependency updates. Given the ease with which AI agents handle web protocols and servers, this technical occupation is under strong pressure towards full automation.

Computer programming is the digital occupation most directly impacted by advances in generative code AI. Currently, AI writes, debugs, and explains code in multiple programming languages with a productivity that multiplies human output. The programmer must evolve towards complex systems architecture and strategic integration, as the coding of standard functions and components is now an algorithmic task.

Recording technicians operate sound and video equipment in studios or on location, integrating digital technology and physical setup. AI already automates equalisation, noise reduction, and audio mixing, as well as intelligent camera tracking in recordings. The technician's value lies in auditory and visual aesthetic judgement, the physical setup of complex microphone arrays, and the resolution of technical problems during live recordings.

These technicians manage the broadcasting of radio and TV signals in high-technology digital environments with service responsibility. AI systems already automate broadcast continuity, signal level control, and scheduled ad insertion. The technical supervision of physical transmission systems and the response to critical outages in national broadcasting maintain the need for a human technician on duty.

Telecommunications engineering technicians install and maintain wireless and cable transmission systems in diverse physical environments. AI monitors network performance and optimises antenna power remotely according to traffic demand. The deployment of 5G/6G infrastructure, the maintenance of communication towers, and the physical repair of faults in the external network protect the manual component of the position.

Accounting clerks perform tasks involving the recording and classification of invoices, expenses, and income in corporate digital systems. AI, through computer vision for reading physical documents and automatic payment processing, already performs 90% of this work autonomously. This is one of the most vulnerable occupations in the administrative sector, as its core tasks are pure logic and handling of structured data that are easily automatable.

Their work consists of managing personnel data and processing payroll based on Spanish labor regulations and collective bargaining agreements. Intelligent payroll systems with AI already automatically update tax withholdings, manage sick leave, and calculate severance payments with minimal oversight. As this is work purely based on logical rules and digital data, the potential for displacement of the human administrative worker is very high.

nombre: Statistical, financial and banking services office clerks
justificacion: These employees handle internal administrative operations in banks and insurance companies, working with purely digital information processes. AI already processes applications for financial products, performs biometric identity verification, and manages international transfers without human errors. A large portion of the banking back-office is being absorbed by intelligent automation systems and risk decision algorithms.

Supply clerks manage inventories and material orders in digital logistics environments and physical warehouses. AI predicts stock needs with pinpoint accuracy based on sales history and automates orders to suppliers autonomously. The physical component of verifying merchandise in the warehouse and managing physical returns is what keeps part of the job protected from analytical AI.

Their work consists of providing administrative support to industrial production, managing work orders and digital manufacturing orders. Enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems assisted by AI already coordinate production schedules and material allocation autonomously. Direct interaction with workers on the physical shop floor and the immediate resolution of manual incidents are the tasks that keep the administrative employee in the plant.

Logistics clerks coordinate passenger and freight transportation through digital fleet management platforms. AI optimizes routes, assigns drivers, and predicts arrival times in real time, surpassing human planning capacity. The employee's value lies in managing physical contingencies (breakdowns, road closures) and providing personal attention to drivers and clients in stressful logistics situations.

These employees perform shelving and lending tasks in libraries and archives, combining digital management with the physical handling of books. AI dominates document classification, automated lending via RFID, and handling simple bibliographic queries through chatbots. The physical arrangement of volumes on shelves and in-person assistance to users in their physical searches partially protect this traditional occupation.

Postal service employees sort and manage the flow of shipments at postal processing centers, a high-volume physical task. AI and sorting robotics already automate address scanning and package separation by routes on a massive scale in Spain. The manual component of handling non-standardized parcels and supervising the physical process at loading docks keeps part of the occupation outside the purely digital realm.

Their work consists of coding information and proofreading texts before printing, a purely digital and linguistic task. Generative AI and advanced grammar-checking tools currently surpass human precision and speed in error detection and text formatting. As this is a task based on linguistic rules and digital orthotypography, the vulnerability to complete automation is extremely high.

These employees manage initial recruitment processes and personnel files in digital human resources departments. AI already performs CV screening, interview scheduling, and automated filing of contracts and legal documentation autonomously. As this is a task centered on managing documents and structured data, the HR administrative employee sees much of their value absorbed by intelligent talent management platforms.

Data entry clerks input information from physical documents into digital systems, a purely mechanical and repetitive task. Currently, optical character recognition (OCR) systems with AI read and digitize any type of document, including handwritten ones, with accuracy exceeding 99%. This occupation is a primary target for total automation, as AI eliminates the need for manual data entry in businesses.

nombre: Administrative clerks without public-facing duties n.e.c.
justificacion: This group encompasses back-office administrative workers who perform document and digital file management tasks without public-facing duties. AI and autonomous software agents can organize folders, draft basic reports, and manage document approval workflows autonomously. Given the purely digital and rule-based nature of their work, the potential for displacement by intelligent automation systems is very high.

Customer information clerks resolve queries and manage administrative procedures in hybrid office and digital service environments. Conversational AI systems and virtual assistants already handle the majority of frequent inquiries autonomously and multilingually. The human employee is reserved for handling complex cases that require empathy, discretionary judgment, or a detailed in-person explanation of Spanish bureaucratic processes.

Receptionists manage visitor reception and external communications in offices, combining physical personal attention with digital tasks. AI already manages visitor registration, biometric access control, and intelligent switchboard systems with great effectiveness. The warmth of in-person greeting, physical entrance security management, and on-the-spot resolution of unforeseen situations maintain the need for a human receptionist.

Their work consists of selling travel packages and designing personalized itineraries, operating in a purely digital environment of tourism information. AI already allows users to search, compare, and book complex trips autonomously through intelligent platforms that learn their preferences. The human travel agent adds value in managing critical cancellations, providing niche expert advice, and offering the reassurance of personal interaction for high-value investments.

Hotel receptionists coordinate guest arrivals and manage services during stays in a physical and social environment. AI already automates digital check-in, mobile key control, and handling standard requests through in-room chatbots. In-person human hospitality, on-site management of complex complaints, and immediate local knowledge for tourist recommendations preserve the professional's value in Spain.

Telephone operators manage high volumes of calls and route communications in digital switchboard environments. Speech recognition and natural language processing (NLP) systems already manage call routing and answer basic queries autonomously. As this is a task based purely on digital voice communication, AI has a very high automation potential, reducing the need for human operators.

The work of a call center agent consists of customer service or telephone sales following pre-established scripts in a digital environment. Conversational AI and realistic voice agents are already capable of maintaining complex dialogues, resolving complaints, and performing mass sales of simple products. This occupation is undergoing massive automation in Spain, as AI drastically reduces operational costs while maintaining a similar quality of service.

Survey agents collect public opinion data through telephone or digital interviews based on structured questionnaires. AI can make thousands of simultaneous calls, analyze voice tone to detect emotions, and transcribe responses automatically and immediately. As this is a repetitive language-based and data-capture task, the role of the human surveyor is being displaced by massive algorithmic efficiency.

Bank tellers perform cash operations and basic administrative tasks for clients in physical offices. The advancement of intelligent ATMs and mobile banking with AI has reduced the need for human intervention for most transactions. The human teller in Spain now focuses on digital education for elderly people and complex transactions requiring physical identity verification and trust.

Their work consists of selling bets and paying out prizes in physical establishments, operating with digital gaming systems. Automated betting terminals and AI-powered online gaming platforms already handle the majority of business volume autonomously and securely. The human employee is necessary for monitoring legal compliance (minors, self-exclusion) and providing in-person service at the physical betting venue.

Casino and gaming hall employees manage tables and machines in physical leisure environments with high social interaction. AI monitors gameplay to detect fraud or addictive behaviors and automates machine payouts digitally. The entertainment component, physical table management (croupiers), and personal hospitality are the areas that protect this occupation from total digitization.

These employees appraise objects and manage pawn loans in a hybrid commercial and technical environment. AI assists in the valuation of standardized goods through computer vision and real-time market price analysis. The physical inspection of the authenticity and condition of unique objects (jewelry, antiques) and personal negotiation with clients in situations of financial need require human judgment and empathy.

nombre: Bill collectors, debt collectors and related
justificacion: Their work consists of collecting debts and recovering outstanding invoices through telephone and digital communication. AI already automates the sending of personalized reminders and makes initial collection calls through voice agents that negotiate simple payment plans. Complex debt recovery and judicial or personal negotiation in cases of critical insolvency continue to depend on the persuasive skill and judgment of a human collector.

Post office counter clerks serve the public, manage shipments, and sell postal products in physical offices. AI facilitates shipment sorting and automates payment and weighing through intelligent self-service terminals. The human component remains for advising on complex international shipments, managing in-person complaints, and performing physical logistics tasks behind the counter in the Spanish postal network.

nombre: Administrative clerks with public-facing duties n.e.c.
justificacion: This group encompasses various public-facing administrative workers who manage procedures and records in physical environments. AI and self-service terminals already enable most standard administrative procedures to be completed without human interaction. The survival of these positions depends on serving citizens with low digital literacy and resolving physical or legal incidents that require on-site human assessment.

These owner-operators combine business management with the physical work of cooking and serving in the restaurant. AI helps with purchasing management and digital marketing for the establishment, but the core of the work is intensely physical and manual. The versatility to cook, wait tables, and solve real operational problems in the establishment protects this occupation due to the lack of affordable and versatile robotics for small businesses.

Cooks perform food preparation and cooking tasks in physical and variable kitchen environments. Although kitchen robotics already automates repetitive processes in fast food (frying, assembling burgers), restaurant cooking requires manual dexterity and sensory judgment. Adapting to the pace of service and the creativity to adjust recipes on the spot based on the quality of fresh produce maintain the value of the human cook.

The work of a waiter consists of attending tables, transporting food and beverages, and managing the customer experience in a physical environment. Service robots exist that transport dishes in controlled environments, but they lack the human agility to navigate obstacles and the charisma for social service. Personal attention, commercial persuasion, and physical management of unexpected situations in the dining area are inherently human skills.

Section managers coordinate sales teams and manage stock in physical retail spaces. AI optimizes inventory and predicts consumer trends automatically, facilitating digital commercial decision-making. Direct supervision of sales staff, on-site resolution of customer conflicts, and maintaining the physical order of the section require in-person leadership that technology cannot replace.

Shop assistants serve customers, advise on products, and manage payment in physical commercial environments. AI dominates online commerce and personalized recommendations, but in-person sales rely on eye contact and empathetic persuasion. The manual component of restocking products and physically helping customers try on garments or examine items protects this occupation from complete automation for now.

Small shop owners manage all aspects of the business, from the physical purchase of merchandise to the final sale. AI allows them to compete through the use of digital management tools and low-cost social media marketing. However, the core of their success in Spain is physical proximity, personal knowledge of the clientele, and the daily manual effort at the point of sale.

Kiosk sales involve proximity-based physical work managing newspapers, lottery tickets, and various items in very small spaces. Digital systems already handle press returns and inventory management automatically. The occupation persists due to the low cost of human labor compared to the complex automation of a physical kiosk and the social and neighborhood service function these positions fulfill.

nombre: Occasional market and flea market sellers
justificacion: Market stall vendors carry out an intensely physical activity of setting up stalls, transporting merchandise, and selling through social bargaining. No AI or robotics technology exists that can operate profitably in the chaotic, outdoor, and variable environment of a traveling street market. Human persuasive ability and physical endurance for working outdoors are the key factors protecting this profession.

Telemarketing work consists of making outbound calls to sell products following strict digital scripts. This occupation is extremely vulnerable to generative voice AI, which can make thousands of simultaneous calls with a persuasive tone of voice and respond to common objections. Regulations against telephone spam and customer preference for human interaction are the only barriers slowing down total automation.

Gas station attendants perform refueling, manage payment, and maintain the station shop in a physical environment. Self-service stations and AI-powered pump payment systems have already automated much of this occupation. The human presence in Spain is maintained for safety reasons, customer services (car wash, air), and to comply with local accessibility regulations.

Door-to-door sales require physical travel, product presentation, and face-to-face commercial persuasion. Although AI helps plan sales routes and segment neighborhoods with the highest potential, closing the sale depends on direct human interaction. The ability to read the social context of a household and generate immediate trust are skills that AI cannot replicate on someone's doorstep.

Sales promoters conduct product demonstrations in shopping centers or physical events to attract customers. Their work is based on charisma, public speaking, and the physical handling of products in front of a live audience. AI can generate promotional avatars on screens, but a human promoter's ability to physically attract the attention of passersby and answer questions on the spot maintains their commercial effectiveness.

Models lend their physical image for advertising and art, working in studio or outdoor environments. Generative AI (GANs and diffusion models) already creates perfect 'virtual models' for catalogs and digital advertising without the need for physical photo sessions. The value of the human model lies in runway shows (live physical events), complex dramatic performance, and the authenticity required by certain luxury or ethical fashion brands.

This group encompasses various salespeople who perform commercial tasks in physical environments or through direct communication channels. AI facilitates lead management and personalization of digital offers, but the execution of the sale usually requires a human close. Physical adaptability and social intelligence to detect sales opportunities in the real world protect these occupations from pure algorithmic automation.

Cashiers and ticket clerks manage payments and issue tickets in high-turnover physical environments. Self-checkout machines, mobile scanning, and AI-powered online ticket sales have already automated much of their work. The human employee is reserved for handling technical payment incidents, verifying ages for alcohol or restricted ticket sales, and providing support to customers with technical difficulties.

Hospital nursing assistants provide direct basic care, patient mobilization, and hygiene in critical physical environments. This is intensely physical work requiring strength, manual delicacy, and constant empathy with suffering patients. Current robotics is far from being able to manipulate sick human bodies with the necessary safety and sensitivity, making this profession essentially in-person and human.

These assistants support primary care centers, performing physical clinical support tasks and direct patient care. Their work includes taking vital signs, dressing simple wounds, and physically accompanying patients during consultations. AI can help with recording medical data, but the physical interaction and emotional support provided to patients in the community setting are tasks that cannot be digitally automated.

Pharmacy assistants support medication management and customer service in the physical setting of the pharmacy. Although AI and robotics automate storage and drug verification, in-person dispensing requires human oversight. Physical assistance to the pharmacist in preparing compounded medications and personal interaction with neighborhood patients ensure the continuity of human work.

Emergency medical technicians operate in unpredictable, high-stress physical environments, performing rescues and first aid. This occupation requires extreme physical strength, the ability to drive in critical situations, and rapid manual decision-making to save lives. AI assists in navigation and communication with the hospital, but physical intervention at the accident scene is exclusively human.

This group encompasses healthcare workers who perform physical support tasks in various healthcare settings. Their functions are eminently manual and in-person, centered on the immediate physical well-being of the people in their care. The variability of human behavior in illness contexts and the need for an empathetic physical response make automation technically and ethically unfeasible in this sector.

Home care workers assist dependent persons with their basic daily activities (hygiene, feeding, mobility) in the home environment. This is purely physical and relational work requiring patience and adaptive manual dexterity that no AI possesses. The loneliness of elderly people in Spain makes the human companionship component as valued as the physical care itself, shielding the profession.

nombre: Childcare workers in nurseries and educational centres
justificacion: Childcare workers in nurseries are responsible for the physical well-being and safety of young children in a physical play and learning environment. The work involves constant tasks of hygiene, feeding, and physical preventive surveillance against accidents. The impossibility of delegating the care of small children to machines for reasons of safety, ethics, and child emotional development ensures the total in-person nature of this human role.

justificacion: Childcare in private homes requires total physical supervision and constant adaptation to the child's needs in a private environment. AI can offer educational games or monitor sleep, but it cannot physically intervene in the face of danger or provide necessary affection. Parents' trust in a human caregiver for the safety of their children at home is an insurmountable barrier to automation.

justificacion: Hairdressing is a purely physical artisanal profession requiring fine manual dexterity and subjective aesthetic judgment in real time. Although AI assists through digital hairstyle simulators, the technical execution of cutting and styling on real human hair is extremely complex for robotics. The social component of hairdressing in Spain, as a space for conversation and trust, reinforces its distinctly human nature.

nombre: Beauty, wellness and related treatment specialists
justificacion: Beauty specialists perform facial and body treatments, manicures, and massages in physical wellness environments. These tasks require a highly developed sense of touch, manual precision, and constant physical adaptation to each client's physiology. AI can help with skin diagnosis, but the manual application of beauty treatments is a physical experience that clients demand from human hands.

nombre: Flight attendants and ship and train stewards
justificacion: Flight attendants and transport stewards manage the safety and comfort of passengers in a physical environment in motion. Although meal service could be partially automated, their primary function is evacuation and first aid in case of emergency. The human presence in the cabin is a legal safety requirement and a factor of psychological reassurance for travelers that AI cannot replace.

nombre: Land transport inspectors and fare collectors
justificacion: Transport inspectors and fare collectors operate in the physical environments of trains and buses, verifying transport tickets and handling incidents. Digital validation systems and automatic turnstiles with AI have already drastically reduced the need for manual collectors. Their current role in Spain focuses on onboard public safety, assistance to passengers with reduced mobility, and manual control in highly complex areas.

Tour escorts guide groups on trips and excursions, handling physical logistics and group engagement. AI can offer audio guides and smart maps, but it cannot manage real contingencies such as transport delays or a traveler's health problems. The ability to create group cohesion and convey passion for the destination are human skills that define the quality of the trip.

Ground crew manage passenger flow at airports and stations, performing check-in and boarding tasks in physical environments. Self-service check-in kiosks and AI facial recognition already perform much of their technical work autonomously. The value of the human professional lies in crisis resolution (canceled flights), assistance to vulnerable persons, and manual management of tense situations in the terminal.

Tour guides explain cultural and natural heritage to visitors in real physical environments. Although AI voice technology and augmented reality offer precise historical explanations, they lack the capacity for improvisation and the emotional narrative of a human guide. The ability to answer unexpected questions and adapt the discourse to the group's interest and energy keeps the human guide as the preferred option for cultural tourism.

nombre: Maintenance and cleaning supervisors in offices, hotels and other establishments
justificacion: These supervisors coordinate cleaning and maintenance teams in large physical establishments, ensuring hygiene and operational readiness. AI assists in shift planning and monitoring the condition of facilities through IoT sensors. The need to perform detailed physical visual inspections and to lead staff in heavy and variable manual tasks ensures the human supervisor maintains an essential operational role.

Butlers manage the complete operation of households or luxury services, combining administrative tasks with direct physical supervision. AI can manage home automation and supplies, but excellence in luxury service depends on human attention to detail and personal discretion. The ability to anticipate the employer's physical and social needs in a private setting requires a very sophisticated social intelligence.

Building caretakers monitor access points, manage correspondence, and perform minor repairs in physical residential or office environments. AI-powered surveillance systems and smart lockers have already automated much of the security and parcel delivery functions. The caretaker figure in Spain endures due to neighborhood trust, the versatility to resolve immediate physical breakdowns, and the human management of community coexistence.

Owners of small rural lodgings or guesthouses physically manage all tasks, from cleaning to guest reception. AI helps them with online marketing and reservation management, allowing them to compete in the digital market. The core of their work is in-person and manual, based on personal hospitality and the constant physical maintenance of facilities in often isolated environments.

Personal companions provide social support and basic physical assistance to people who live alone or have difficulties. This occupation is purely human and in-person, focused on combating loneliness and ensuring the physical safety of the assisted person. AI can offer voice interaction (smart assistants), but it lacks the physical contact, affection, and real intervention capacity that define this human companionship work.

justificacion: Funeral home employees perform physical tasks of preparing the deceased and managing ceremonies in environments of high emotional sensitivity. Animal caretakers and trainers work in direct physical contact with living beings in variable environments such as clinics, zoos, or training centers. Handling animals requires an intuitive reading of physical behavior and a quick manual response to ensure safety and welfare. AI is useful for monitoring animal health through data, but training and direct care are irreplaceable human physical tasks.

justificacion: Driving instructors teach vehicle operation in real traffic environments, physically supervising the student's actions in the car. Although AI-powered driving simulators are advanced, teaching in the real world requires constant physical vigilance to prevent accidents. The ability to convey calm and to intervene on the vehicle controls in milliseconds ensures the need for an in-person human instructor.

Astrologers and fortune tellers offer advice based on spiritual beliefs through direct personal interaction with their clients. Although AI systems exist that generate automated horoscopes and tarot readings, the value of the service lies in the mystical and emotional human connection. Demand in this sector is based on the search for personal meaning, something clients rarely accept from a purely logical machine.

This heterogeneous group of personal service workers performs highly varied manual and physical accompaniment tasks. Their functions are eminently in-person and depend on direct physical interaction with people or objects in the real world. As these are low-digital-level tasks with high operational variability, AI does not offer cost-effective automation solutions, keeping these occupations as refuges for human manual labor.
